title: It’s alright
content: Been a hatch user for approx 2 years. I’ve had a premium subscription the entire time but am planning to cancel it. I like the clock, but have very disappointed with the app for the past 6 months. App is super laggy and the ux isn’t great. Also no clue why but the company used to be so good with adding new sounds/morning moments/alarms. Over the past year they’ve gotten rid of a lot of the audio content and have added maybe 3 new alarms ???? I was willing to pay for these past few years but it makes no sense to pay when they continuously remove or don’t update their library… (@hatch pls bring back that coach that hypes you for the day). And there’s minor things that I’m surprised haven’t been improved. For example, there’s no way to temporarily toggle off your evening cue for a day. You have to turn it off completely and remember to turn it back on the next day. Lastly, the free version should not be so bare bones for an over $200 clock. It’s one thing to not have all the sleep and wake options to listen to, but why the heck can’t we choose the colour of the light without paying $60 CAD each year? So tl;dr it’s a predatory subscription model and the subscription has gotten much worse over the year. Consider any other sunrise clock.

title: Very bad design issues.
content: I purchased the camera first and after spending several minutes trying to connect it for the first time I put it back in the box and returned it. There’s absolutely no excuse in 2026 for something as simple as connecting a device over WiFi or Bluetooth to be so problematic. I mean Nest is not great, but usually takes me 5 minutes to have a camera online. Anyway, two months later I got the Dream sock as a present and the same thing happened, impossible to get the thing setup because it won’t connect to my home network or Bluetooth. A quick look at Reddit tells me this is a well known issue with Owlet products. I’m a technical person and even for me troubleshooting this is a nightmare. I know if I invest the time I will eventually get it to work. But why? This is core, basic feature that should be pretty straightforward. Owlet should issue a recall. Something obviously went very wrong in the development of these products. Customer should not be blindsided like this into buying defective products.

title: Arbitrary Limitations with Restore 3
content: The sunrise alarm itself is so wonderful and effective BUT this app is very limited even with the expensive subscription. Pros: - creating nightly routines - sunrise alarm (with the subscription, it gradually gets brighter over the course of one hour before my alarm sound goes off) - nice alarm sound options (im usually awake by the time this sounds, so this functions more as a transition to getting out of bed) Cons - youre limited to create two nightly routines. Having 7 nights a week and many choices for sounds, its surprising there arent more routines available - routines are limited to nighttime only (i was looking to create a morning routine to stack morning sounds to ease the transition out of bed but you cant even select the morning sounds) (want to go sunrise-> birdsong -> stretching/movement/music) - night and morning sounds cannot be interchanged (i enjoy a music option in the night category and would like to use it in the morning but cannot) - the alarm will stay on for one hour if i dont turn it off then it automatically shuts off (i should have a choice over this in terms of length and option to shut off) - i tried to create a workaround with the morning routine by creating a second alarm to go off after my initial alarm sounds but because the first alarm could last 1 hour, the app doesnt let me set a second alarm until after this 1 hour passes - the app is organized rather poorly, theres no way to see everything in one place or to see whats a premium feature that im paying for (beyond the 1 month trial) General grievances with the company - its impossible to get a hold of anyone online. I used the chat feature on the website which sent me in circles to pages that dont exist - the chat ai offered me a link to be a beta tester after talking to it about the restore 3, and theres no restore 3 listed in the options - their website is also poorly organized for support. Theres no one page, one video, or one document manual with everything you need to operate the app, its all buried under case by case issues with very limited support. title: Clunky and stagnant feature development
content: I bought the original Hatch Restore many years ago and although the app was a bit clunky, I saw great potential with the high quality hardware. I assumed the app would continue to improve over time with updates that give it more functionality and UX improvements. However, for the past 5 years, I haven’t seen any meaningful updates to the app. It’s frustrating because here’s so much potential to make a great product here but the tech is stuck 5-10 years in the past. A few notable misses: 1. No integration with home automation systems (HomeKit, Google Home, Alexa, etc). For a smart device, this is the bare minimum in 2026. 2. The device has a speaker, but can’t be used as a wifi speaker. (Probably to force more people into a subscription but you’re losing on customer acquisition by omitting this functionality.) 3. Only one wind-down routine can be configured. Why can’t I have more than one and why can’t it be a gradual dimming, like a sunset? 4. No option to gradually increase volume in parallel with the light intensity. You can only pick one volume level for the sound. It’s impossible to recreate the atmosphere of a sunrise in the forest where you hear one little bird and then more gradually start chirping as the sun comes up. It would be so easy to do this! 5. The custom app UI is clunky and dated. Just use the standard Apple UI components with a theme. It’ll save dev time and be consistent UX for users 6. The subscription should have more value for what it costs. If you’re going to charge a recurring fee for content, it should have new, high quality content daily. Look at Headspace for guidance. Since I’ve bought my Hatch Restore there have been 2 new versions of it released, but my experience with the Hatch Restore 1 hasn’t given me much enthusiasm to consider upgrading.

I think it's very clear they put more effort and documentation for iOS users instead of Android. The ability to do anything on this app is sub par at best and nothing matches their documentation for any procedure you might need to do. It's a garbage user experience at best.

title: I LOVE the hatch. I HATE the app
content: I’m so frustrated with the app 9/10 times. I love the hatch. It’s been an absolute life saver and I buy it for every friend having a baby. My daughter is 3.5 years old and we still use it for naps and bedtime. But the app needs some serious updates!! The “next” and “back” button and greyed out so I only knew they existed by reaching out to customer service long ago. They advised removing and reinstalling the app. Did that… no help. Then I have been paying for the monthly subscription to get the bedtime story songs. You can add them to a playlist but you can’t make it play a specific song unless you “back/next” to find the specific song you want. And while that doesn’t sound like the worst thing, if you have a picky toddler like me, it is. It has almost made me consider cancelling the monthly subscription and finding something else because my toddler only wants these two specific stories. And then she wakes up many times asking for it to go back to bed. So I spend several minutes hitting “back/next” until I can get to it. And the feature is less than usable. It often doesn’t register when you press one of those two buttons, and even when it does, doesn’t always work. So insanely frustrating. Especially in the sleep regressions because it means I spend a ton of time on my phone while I’d much prefer going back to bed. It is still a huge help though so we do keep it around and I love that it was a total game changer for her sleeping when she was a baby. We got it when she was about 4 months old and it basically runs non stop day and night since then and still works absolutely perfect minus the app issues I have with it. Absolutely still highly recommend it- just seriously wish the company would fix the app features I mentioned. I already reached out to them a couple of years ago about the other app issues and they did fix those features within about a month so that was nice. Wish they would also add more bedtime story songs. The other types of bed songs they offer in different categories are almost too much high tones or excitement. Love the monotone type stories with some songs in the same. When you add a bedtime story song to a playlist- it’s not really adding THAT song. It’s adding the “bedtime story song playlist” to the schedule/playlist. So fixing that would be helpful. Maybe add the option to play that single song everytime you select a specific schedule or a feature that allows you the option to rotate the ones. But it is annoying that I have to scroll everytime when I select a schedule/playlist.

title: Bonne app mais attention aux bugs qui doivent être corrigés
content: L’app fonctionne bien dans l’ensemble, organisation cohérente et user friendly. Cependant en tant qu’utilisateur avancé voici ce que je peux signaler aux devs: - le principe d’avoir 2 versions de l’app (owlet care et owlet dream) alors que ce sont exactement les mêmes, juste une histoire de « région » d’utilisation, c’est aberrant. Changez ça. - Quand on reçoit une notification jaune (chaussette mal placée ou mauvaise réception de données), il est très fréquent que la notification s’ouvre (ce qui ouvre l’application), le message d’avertissement (jaune) s’ouvre ensuite et quand on clique sur « OK » ça tourne en boucle. Obligé de quitter l’app puis de la relancer pour pouvoir enfin accéder à l’application (et pouvoir aller désactiver la surveillance temporairement par ex). Ce bug est vraiment récurent et agaçant (peut être est-il corrigé dans la dernière version?). - Dans la même série que le bug précédent, parfois le panneau jaune disparaît après avoir cliqué sur OK mais le bouton pour désactiver la station reste bloqué « en chargement » et il n’est pas désactivable (même après plusieurs secondes…). Obligé de relancer l’app à nouveau. - Je n’ai pas vu de paramètres sur le délai de stockage des clips vidéos? Aussi, il serait bien de pouvoir faire en sorte que les clips vidéos soient archivables sur un périphérique local comme un partage SMB (ex: NAS) sur le même wifi. On a aucun moyen d’archiver les vidéos pour le moment, tout reste dans votre cloud uniquement…. - Avoir la possibilité de gérer facilement le fait de connecter la base (voir même la caméra) sur plusieurs réseaux wifi, ce qui serait idéal pour pouvoir déplacer facilement la base et la chaussette dans des endroits où on se rend souvent (nounou, grand mère…) sans avoir à re-parametrer. En l’état ça ne fonctionne qu’à notre domicile et c’est vraiment dommage - le voyant vert qui scintille sur la base quand tout va bien est vraiment trop faible en intensité, parfois on dirait que la base est éteinte. - le dev d’une compatibilité avec les widgets pour avoir un aperçu immédiat de la fréquence cardiaque / respiration et même des alertes devrait être réalisé, ce serait vraiment un plus. De la même manière qu’une app compatible Apple Watch.
